_comment: Docket workbench config. This folder is portable - copy it anywhere. The working project sits BESIDE it, never inside it.
_python: PIN THE ABSOLUTE VENV PATH. Run `which python` with your venv active and paste it here. Spawned scripts do NOT inherit an activated venv - they get whatever VS Code launched with. A bare 'python' means the mutation gate dies with 'command not found' and the dev agent hunts a bug that isn't there.
_project: Active sibling project folder name. Set by 'Docket: Select Project' or 'Docket: Clone Project'. null = ask on first run.
_models: null = resolve at runtime by role. Only pin a family string if you have a reason; a wrong string makes selectChatModels return an empty array, which fails silently.

governor._fan_out: Plans are cheap (~6k tokens); a wrong plan that runs to QA and back is ~200k. Fan out only when risky: unknowns were non-empty but recoverable, or the touched modules have a bad history in the ledger.

security_snyk._note: Flip on once the Snyk binary and service account exist.

jira._ac_field_ids: Comma-separated customfield IDs to check FIRST for acceptance criteria, e.g. 'customfield_10300'. null = search: scan custom fields, then rendered fields, then an 'Acceptance Criteria' heading in the description. Jira does not return field NAMES in the issue payload, which is why this is a search and not a setting. Run `python scripts/jira_fetch.py PROJ-110` to see which tier found yours, then pin it here to skip the search.
jira._note: Env var NAMES, never values. Nothing secret lives in this file. Put JIRA_BASE_URL and JIRA_PAT in your environment, or in <workbench>/.local/docket-runtime.env (gitignored).

ledger._local: Relative to this workbench folder. ONE ledger serves every sibling project - the 'project' column separates them, and cross-project org findings come free.
ledger._never_share: Do NOT point this at a network share. SQLite's WAL mode does not work over NFS/SMB and locking there is broken - that is a data-loss incident with a countdown. Events are append-only, so federating later is an export/import, not a shared file. Every run records its origin (user@host) so ledgers can be merged without id collisions.
